ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION 503, DEFENDANT CARRIER CORPORATION'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT, OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE, SUMMARY ADJUDICATION. HEARING HELD AND TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED AS REVISED: DEFENDANT CARRIER CORPORATION'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT AND ALTERNATE MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION ARE DENIED. DEFENDANT FAILED TO SUSTAIN ITS INITIAL BURDEN OF DEMONSTRATING THAT PLAINTIFF DOES NOT POSSESS AND CANNOT REASONABLY OBTAIN EVIDENCE THAT HE WAS EXPOSED TO ASBESTOS-CONTAINING PRODUCTS OR MATERIALS ATTRIBUTABLE TO DEFENDANT. PLAINTIFF'S DEPOSITION TESTIMONY AND RESPONSES TO DEFENDANT'S SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES PRECLUDE DEFENDANT FROM SUCCESSFULLY INVOKING THE FACTUALLY DEVOID PRONG OF AGUILAR. EVEN IF DEFENDANT HAD SUSTAINED ITS BURDEN, PLAINTIFF'S DECLARATION CREATES A TRIABLE ISSUE WHETHER HE WAS EXPOSED TO ASBESTOS-CONTAINING PRODUCTS OR MATERIALS (GASKETS) ATTRIBUTABLE TO DEFENDANT. DEFENDANT'S ALTERNATE MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS DENIED FOR VIOLATION OF CRC 3.1350(D). EVEN IF THE COURT CONSIDERED DEFENDANT'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION OF PLAINTIFF'S NEGLIGENCE AND STRICT LIABILITY CAUSES OF ACTION, IT WOULD BE DENIED. IT WOULD BE DENIED AS TO NEGLIGENCE, STRICT LIABILITY, FOR THE SAME REASONS SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS DENIED. DEFENDANT CARRIER'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION AS TO PLAINTIFF'S CAUSE OF ACTION FOR FALSE REPRESENTATION AND CLAIM FOR PUNITIVE DAMAGES ARE WITHDRAWN. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: TERI L. JACKSON, REPORTER: PATRICIA RODRIGUEZ OF AIKEN WELCH, CSR #12436

ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION 503, DEFENDANT FLOWSERVE CORPORATION SUCCESSOR-IN-INTEREST TO DURAMETALLIC CORPORATION, INCORRECTLY NAMED AS DURAMETALLIC CORPORATION'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT AND/OR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION. HEARING HELD AND TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED: DEFENDANT FLOWSERVE CORPORATION SUCCESSOR-IN INTEREST TO DURAMETALLIC CORPORATION, INCORRECTLY NAMED AS DURAMETALLIC CORPORATION'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS GRANTED. DEFENDANT SUSTAINED ITS INITIAL BURDEN. PLAINTIFF FAILED TO PRESENT EVIDENCE TO CREATE A TRIABLE ISSUE WHETHER HE WAS EXPOSED TO ASBESTOS-CONTAINING PRODUCTS OR MATERIALS ATTRIBUTABLE TO DEFENDANT. DEFENDANT'S OBJECTIONS TO PLAINTIFF'S DECLARATION ARE SUSTAINED. DEFENDANT'S OBJECTIONS TO MR. AY'S DECLARATION ARE SUSTAINED. JUDGE: TERI L. MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION
Off Calendar Jan-29-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Eric D. Sentlinger of DEHAY ELLISTON LLP (510-285-0750), appearing for defendant / moving party Kaiser Gypsum Company, Inc. and Hanson Permanente Cement, Inc. (fka Kasier Cement Corporation)
Jennifer L. Alesio of BRAYTON & PURCELL (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiff / opposing party Edward Shortall

RULING:
DEFENDANTS KAISER GYPSUM COMPANY, INC. AND HANSON PERMANENTE CEMENT, INC. (FKA KASIER CEMENT CORPORATION)'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION

Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted: Defendants Kaiser Gypsum Company, Inc. and Hanson Permanente Cement, Inc. (fka Kasier Cement Corporation)'s motion for summary adjudication of plaintiff's claim for punitive damages is denied. Defendants failed to sustain their initial burden under either prong of Aguilar. Defendant failed to negate an essential element of plaintiff's claim for punitive damages. Defendant failed to sustain its burden of demonstrating that plaintiff does not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that defendants acted with the requisite malice, fraud or oppression to warrant the imposition of punitive damages. Plaintiff's responses to defendants' special interrogatories preclude defendants from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. Order signed in open court. Judge: Teri L. Jackson, Reporter: Patricia Rodriguez of AIKEN WELCH, CSR #12436 (Tel: 510-451-1580) (503/TLJ)

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Jan-17-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Sara K. Egide of TUCKER ELLIS LLP (415-617-2219), appearing for defendant / moving party Carrier Corporation
Anne T. Acuna of BRAYTON & PURCELL (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiff / opposing party Edward Shortall

RULING:
DEFENDANT CARRIER CORPORATION'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT, OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE, SUMMARY ADJUDICATION

Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted as revised: Defendant Carrier Corporation's motion for summary judgment and alternate motion for summary adjudication are denied. Defendant failed to sustain its initial burden of demonstrating that plaintiff does not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that he was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. Plaintiff's deposition testimony and responses to defendant's special interrogatories preclude defendant from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. Even if defendant had sustained its burden, plaintiff's declaration creates a triable issue whether he was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials (gaskets) attributable to defendant. Defendant's alternate motion for summary adjudication is denied for violation of CRC 3.1350(d). Even if the court considered defendant's motion for summary adjudication of plaintiff's negligence and strict liability causes of action, it would be denied. It would be denied as to negligence, strict liability, for the same reasons summary judgment is denied. Defendant Carrier's motion for summary adjudication as to plaintiff's cause of action for false representation and claim for punitive damages are withdrawn. Order signed in open court. Judge: Teri L. Jackson, Reporter: Patricia Rodriguez of AIKEN WELCH, CSR #12436 (Tel: 510-451-1580) (503/TLJ)

Anne T. Acuna of BRAYTON & PURCELL (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiff / opposing party Edward Shortall

RULING:
DEFENDANT RELIABLE PIPE SUPPLY COMPANY, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR, IN THE ALTERNATIVE, SUMMARY ADJUDICATION

Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Reliable Pipe Supply Company, Inc.'s motion for summary judgment is denied and its alternate motion for summary adjudication is denied as to issue 1 (negligence and strict liability) and off calendar as to issues 2, 3 and 4 (false misrepresentation, premises owner/contractor liability and punitive damages). Defendant sustained its initial burden. Plaintiff's deposition testimony and declaration and Mr. Ay's declaration create a triable issue whether plaintiff was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials (insulation inserts) attributable to defendant. Defendant's objections to the declarations of plaintiff and Mr. Ay are overruled. Plaintiff has dismissed his false misrepresentation and premises owner/contractor liability causes of action and claim for punitive damages. Order signed in open court. Judge: Teri L. Jackson, Reporter: Patricia Rodriguez of AIKEN WELCH, CSR #12436 (Tel: 510-451-1580) (503/TLJ)

MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANTS CAHILL CONTRACTORS, INC., CAHILL CONSTRUCTION CO., INC. AND CAHILL CONSTRUCTION SERVICES, INC.'S MOTION TO STRIKE PORTIONS OF PLAINTIFFS' THIRD AMENDED COMPLAINT.
Appearances: Cesar Alvarado of Archer Norris (925.930.6600) for defendants and moving parties; Oren Noah of Brayton Purcell (898.1555) for plaintiff and responding party.
Argued and the court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: denied. Plaintiffs' complaint - the summary complaint and Brayton Purcell's master complaint - states allegations sufficient to properly plead a claim for punitive damages. The court did not consider the deposition testimony of Darryl Diamond, which both parties attached. The grounds for a motion to strike must appear either on the face of the challenged pleading or from matters which the court may judicially notice. (CCP 437.) Order signed. Judge: Teri L. Christopher Hersom, Esq., of BRAYTON & PURCELL (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiff / opposing party Edward Shortall

RULING:
DEFENDANT HAJOCA CORPORATION'S MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT

Argued and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Hajoca Corporation's motion to strike portions of plaintiff's complaint is denied as to requests to strike the amendment substituting it as DOE 40 and taken off calendar as to the remained of the motion as moot in light of the tentative ruling on defendant's demurrer. Defendant's arguments about the untimeliness and insufficiency of the amendment substituting it as DOE 40 is, in practical effect, a motion for summary judgment either on grounds of the defenses of statute of limitations and laches and should, if at all, be made that way instead of a motion to strike. Order signed in open court. Judge: Harold Kahn, Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR # 7077 = (220/HEK)

RULING:
DEFENDANT NIBCO INC.'S MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT

While it may be rue that plaintiff may add Nibco as a defendant through other means, the sole issue on this motion is whether plaintiff may add NIBCO per CCP 474.
